title: cannabis sativa subsp. intersita
intro: Cannabis sativa subsp. intersita, also known as Indian hemp, is a subspecies of the Cannabis sativa plant, which is a plant species that has been cultivated for its medicinal, recreational, and industrial uses for thousands of years. Research has shown that Cannabis sativa subsp. intersita contains a range of medicinal properties, including cannabinoids and terpenes, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticonvulsant effects. The health benefits of Cannabis sativa subsp. intersita include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, chronic pain, and sleep disorders, as well as its ability to stimulate appetite and improve overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Cannabis sativa subsp. intersita include dried flowers, leaves, and extracts, which can be used in teas, tinctures, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
